summary: Yes, mesopic pupil size can be measured using cobalt blue light slit-lamp biomicroscopy because it allows detailed retinal examination under controlled illumination during low-light conditions. The technique enables accurate assessment by adjusting brightness and focusing tools suited for smaller or partially dilated pupils typical in mesoptic environments.
